title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for http/1.1 response serialization, field order, framing, and writes and requires the smallest strict canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.
Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Response Commit Order
steps.instruction:
Order the Response Commit Order evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Response Commit Order.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Response Commit Order assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Response Commit Order, keep Response Commit Order connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A quiz protocol team handles case 7093 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Response Commit Order has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
